original_abstract: STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) education is an alternative learning approach to constructing 21st century skills.It encourages the emergence of innovation to develop these skills.The study uses a systematic literature review method adapted from Sharif and Moher et al. using PRISMA.The content analysis aimed to systematically analyze trends in STEM or STEAM education research in Indonesia using articles published in national and international journals.From the inclusion criteria, there were 63 articles collected analyzed using the Paper Classification Form (PCF) instrument adapted from Kizilaslan et al. with indicators title, author, author nation, journal name, year publishing, journal type, language, index, principal discipline, research method, data collection, sample, and data analysis.The findings indicate that the dominant topic is learning strategies with experimental research methods.STEM or STEAM education dominates at the secondary school level for science subjects.Project-based learning, problem-based learning, and Inquiry are the dominant learning models integrated with STEM or STEM learning.Critical thinking ability, scientific literacy, and learning outcomes are the dominant theme in STEM or STEAM education.Empirical research is needed on the potential of STEAM education to improve skills in 21st-century learning.
humanized_abstract: STEM or STEAM education is one of the approaches to develop 21st-century skills. It fosters the creativity to develop the skills. This study uses systematic literature review method inspired by the work of Sharif and Moher et al. using PRISMA. The content analysis in this study aims to identify trends of STEM or STEAM education research in Indonesia by analyzing articles from both local and international journals. Total of 63 articles were collected based on the inclusion criteria and then analyzed using Paper Classification Form (PCF) instrument adapted from Kizilaslan et al. which has indicators of title, author, author nation, journal name, year of publication, journal type, language, index, main discipline, research method, data collection, sample, and data analysis. Based on the analysis, the dominating topic is learning strategies with experimental research method. STEM or STEAM education has been developed in secondary schools, especially for science subjects. The dominating learning models are project-based learning, problem-based learning, and Inquiry integrated with STEM or STEM learning. The dominating theme of critical thinking skill, scientific literacy, and learning outcomes are dominating in STEM or STEAM education. Further empirical research are needed to investigate the potentials of STEAM education to develop 21st-century learning skills.
